COOKIES_POPUP.POPUP_CONTENT_HTML:
This website uses cookies, including third-party cookies, to optimize the functionality of the website and for website analysis and statistics.
Find Out More

ALABERN_PORTAL.MAIN_TITLE_HTML:
The Alabern portal.
Photography in
Catalonia
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_TITLE_HTML: A project of the Generalitat de Catalunya managed by the Museu Nacional d’Art de Catalunya
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_CONTENT_1_HTML:
Ever since photography arrived in Catalonia, it has been a prime means of expression that has documented society’s transformation and served as a vehicle for creativity.

The Government of Catalonia’s National Photography Plan was the seed of Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya, the first online portal which serves as a comprehensive compilation of Catalonia’s photographic culture. A project like this one is only possible thanks to the collaboration of many institutions, private collections and the photographers themselves, who have chosen the best from their own collections and oeuvres to present it publicly, all in one location. The website also provides an overview of the activities and extensive resources on photography, both documentary and educational, available all over Catalonia.
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_CONTENT_2_HTML:
The main goals of Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya are to share and disseminate the historical and contemporary photographic heritage both nationally and internationally, and to become a digital window into the richness, quality and diversity of this world. The portal aims to foster society’s appreciation of photographic culture by covering the information and services needed regarding activities associated with this field and new developments that arise in the fields of documentary, creative and heritage photography.

Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya aims to:
  1. Offer a useful, effective platform that allows for searches of Catalan photography collections all in one place, as well as providing centralised access to information on Catalonia’s photographic heritage, photography theory, preventive conservation and legal aspects affecting the use of photographs, thus covering the main areas comprising photographic culture.
  2. Encourage the use of heritage photography in scholarly research by carving out avenues that connect it with the social sciences.
  3. Promote the habitual use of heritage and creative photography in formal and informal educational settings at schools and learning centres.
  4. Create a community around photographic culture to foster an increasing sense of belonging among citizens, who use this platform to express themselves and interact via photographic culture.
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_CONTENT_3_HTML: The logo of the website Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya aims to pay explicit tribute to the figure of Ramon Alabern, the engraver and photographer who made the first photographic image in Spain on 10 November 1839. It was in Barcelona, in front of the Llotja, at a public event where the resulting daguerreotype, whose whereabouts is unknown today, was awarded to one of the attendees via a drawing.
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_CONTENT_4_HTML: A group of experts have chosen a catalogue of 600 photographers who are seminal in the field of photography in Catalonia with the goal of covering all periods and formats, the prime criterion being quality. This list will be expanded year after year with the participation of other experts and the institutions participating in the project.
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_TITLE_5: Photographers chosen for the list on the Photography Portal of Catalonia
ALABERN_PORTAL.SUB_CONTENT_5_HTML:
1. Photographers with works in public collections and holdings in Catalonia coming from either acquisitions and donations or deposits.
  2. Works by unidentified photographers in public collections and holdings in Catalonia coming from either acquisitions and donations or deposits, which were registered as «Unidentified photographer».
  3. Photographers in private collections, archives or holdings with a public facet, such as associations, foundations, centres, etc.
  4. Catalan photographers whose works have been published in books, catalogues, other publications, studies, academic theses, etc.
  5. Catalan photographers with entries in prominent dictionaries and/or encyclopaedias, both national and international.
  6. Catalan photographers with works in public collections and archives outside Catalonia.
  7. Photographers whose works describe, document or interpret important times in the evolution and transformation of different facets of Catalonia: political, territorial, social, technical, scientific, artistic, architectural, urbanistic, humanistic, etc.
  8. Photographers who were pioneers in developing photographic techniques or procedures, who have clearly contributed to them and who have applied them in their works.
  9. Photographers who have developed and applied innovative visual, aesthetic and expressive resources.
  10. Photographers who have launched different movements throughout the history of photography, from its inception until now, or who belonged to or played a prominent role in such movements.

PHOTOGRAPHY_PLAN.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
The National Photography Plan was created with the goal of generating a comprehensive, organised institutional policy to promote photography and Catalonia’s photographic heritage, and to ensure that photography is identified as a source of creative expression and document of historical events and recognised as a discipline in which Catalonia has excelled.

The mission of the National Photography Plan of Catalonia is to normalise, coordinate and promote actions that have been and will be developed in Catalonia in the field of photography, reflecting the different sensibilities of the stakeholders involved in managing Catalonia’s photographic heritage, both historical and contemporary.

NEW_ACQUISTIONS.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
The National Photography Collection is part of the National Photography Plan’s action plans and is being created with the prime goal of overseeing Catalonia’s cultural and artistic photographic capital in order to ensure that the most significant auteur photographic expressions in Catalonia are present, protected and valued.

Based on an analysis of the existing holdings in public collections, the Government of Catalonia has been acquiring works for the National Collection since 2015, and in 2017 it started an annual call for submissions so that artists, gallery owners or collectors could submit their own proposals.

The content of the collection reflects the most important contributions acquired by this means, while also taking into account photographic projects carried out in Catalonia, which constitute both a contribution to the photographic language and a reflection on the culture, society or territory of Catalonia.

PHOTO_SEARCH_ENGINE.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya is the first website that both compiles and disseminates Catalonia’s photographic heritage. When browsing the catalogue, you can search by photographer, technique, institution, date the photograph was taken or copyright. The search engines allows you to both locate specific items and discover new contents associated with them. Explore a living legacy which will continue to grow with the addition of new photographs, institutions, collections and photographers.

ART_WORK_FINDER.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: Delve into the photography collections of the institutions participating in Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya. In this directory, you can interrelate the documented materials, see them as a whole and discover them as a source of inspiration for both research and creation.

PHOTOGRAPHER_SEARCH_ENGINE.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: Welcome to the photographer search engine, a space that contextualises and compiles a wide variety of authors from Catalonia’s photographic heritage. Search, discover and get inspired by a catalogue that connects the historical past with contemporary photography. Each file contains information on the photographers, including a detailed biography and their oeuvre, which is connected to the artistic and creative currents of each period.

INSTITUTION_SEARCHER.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya is a repository fed by the collections of many archives, museums and libraries, as well as photographers themselves, who have chosen the best of their work to share with the public. To preserve and share this heritage, we continue working to get new participants to join the project. In this search engine you will find the participating institutions.

ALL_RESOURCES.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya promotes the research, knowledge and dissemination of photography. This is why it also compiles a range of documentary and bibliographic resources on theory, preventive conservation and legal aspects related to the practice of photography. Check the catalogue of publications that complements and expands on the photographic repository.

CALENDAR_SEARCH.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: Photography enables us to feel excitement, share experiences, generate knowledge and interact with our heritage. Check our calendar to keep abreast of new developments in the fields of documentary, creative and heritage photography. You’ll find a wide range of activities targeted at all audiences.

BEHIND_THE_CAMERA.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: We invite you to discover a repository of photography-related contents. Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya was created with the goal of becoming a digital window into the richness, quality and diversity of Catalonia’s photographic heritage. Check the resources and materials, which are constantly being updated, that will deepen your engagement with the collection through new stories and perspectives.

VIRTUAL_EXHIBITION.NO_DATA_MESSAGE_1: We are updating the contents of this page.

WHO_WE_ARE.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
This website is one of the outcomes of the National Photography Plan promoted by the Ministry of Culture since December 2014 in accordance with the Government Agreement on this matter.

It was developed via a group effort that merged the conclusions of previous initiatives, such as the 2011-2012 document drafted by the Expert Group for the Analysis of Catalonia’s Photographic Heritage created by the Directorate General of Archives, Libraries, Museums and Heritage, and the CoNCA report on the state of the photography sector.
WHO_WE_ARE.SECTION_2_CONTENT_HTML: Each institution or artist provided the documentation on each image, and the team developing the website completed and standardised this information, when needed. We are currently working with other institutions to improve photography documentation standards. One of the first outcomes of this effort is the thesaurus on photographic techniques included in the Tesaurus d'Art i Arquitectura (Art and Architecture Thesaurus).
WHO_WE_ARE.SECTION_3_PANNEL_1_TITLE: Initial team who drew up the list of photographers

  • Laura Terré. Photography historian

WHO_WE_ARE.SECTION_3_PANNEL_5_TITLE: Editorial Council of Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya
WHO_WE_ARE.SECTION_3_PANNEL_5_CONTENT_HTML:
The Photography in Catalonia website has an editorial council which meets periodically to advise on its contents. The editorial council is currently made up of:

  • Pepe Serra, Director of the Museu Nacional d’Art de Catalunya
  • Roser Cambray, Curator in the Photography Department of the Museu Nacional d’Art de Catalunya
  • Milena Pi, Coordinator of the Alabern. Fotografia a Catalunya project
  • Mari Santos Garcia Felguera, art historian, exhibition curator and university professor
  • Marta Dahó art historian, exhibition curator and university professor
  • Pedro Vicente, PhD in photography, publisher, exhibition curator and university professor
  • Pep Rigol, photographer and cultural manager

ACCESSIBILITY.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
The Consorci del Museu Nacional d'Art de Catalunya has committed to making its website accessible in accordance with Royal Decree 1112/2018, of September 7, on the accessibility of websites and applications for mobile devices in the public sector (hereinafter Royal Decree 1112/2018, of September 7).
This accessibility statement applies to https://www.fotografiacatalunya.cat

Compliance status
This website is partially compliant with Royal Decree 1112/2018, of September 7 due to the exceptions indicated below.

Content not accessible
The content listed below is not accessible for the following reasons: lack of conformity with Royal Decree 1112/2018, of September 7.
  • Non-textual content
  • Information and relationships
  • Guidance
  • Identify the field purpose
  • Contrast (minimum)
  • Resizing the text
  • Reflow
  • Spacing in the text
  • Keyboard
  • Avoid blocks
  • Title of pages
  • Purpose of links (in context)
  • Multiple ways
  • Visible focus
  • Consistent browsing
  • Identification of errors
  • Labels or instructions
  • Suggestions for errors
  • Processing
  • Name, function, value
  • Status messages
Preparation of this accessibility statement
This statement was prepared on 9/21/2020.
The method used to prepare the declaration has been self-assessment with external resources, according to article 3, paragraph 1, of Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2018/1523 (2).
Last revision of the statement: 21/9/2020.

Observations and contact details
Communications
You can make communications about accessibility requirements (article 10.2.a of Royal Decree 1112/2018), through the following contact mailbox
Per exemple:
  • Informar sobre qualsevol possible incompliment per part d’aquest lloc web.
  • Informar sobre qualsevol possible incompliment per part d’aquest lloc web.
  • Informar sobre qualsevol possible incompliment per part d’aquest lloc web.
Les comunicacions seran rebudes i traslladades per la Direcció General d’Atenció Ciutadana, del Departament de la Vicepresidència, Economia i Hisenda, com a Unitat Responsable d’Accessibilitat Web en l’àmbit autonòmic.
Requests for accessible information and complaints

To make requests for accessible information and complaints (article 12 of Royal Decree 1112/2018) it is necessary to access the procedure “Accessible content on the web” and follow the mode “Request accessible information or make a complaint”

Any natural or legal person can make complaints regarding compliance with the requirements of Royal Decree 1112/2018 and request information regarding content that is excluded from the scope of application of this regulation.
Requests and complaints are registered in accordance with the requirements established by Law 39/2015 of October 1, on the Common Administrative Procedure of Public Administrations.

Application procedure
The application procedure set out in article 13 of Royal Decree 1112/2018 can be started in the “Contents accessible on the web” procedure through the “Reclaim a request” mode.

If, once a request for accessible information or a complaint has been made, it has been rejected, you do not agree with the decision taken or the response does not meet the requirements of article 12.5 of RD 1112/2018, you can initiate a claim for
  • know and oppose the reasons for rejection
  • urge the adoption of the appropriate measures in case of disagreement with the decision adopted
  • state the reasons why you consider that the answer does not meet the required requirements.
A complaint can also be initiated if 20 working days have passed without obtaining any response.

LEGAL_NOTICE.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
All photographs displayed on the portal have been provided by the participating institutions or their authors or holders of intellectual property rights. The use of images on the portal is limited to personal use, for teaching or for research in accordance with the current applicable law, except for records where there is expressly a license to use with certain conditions or in cases where it is indicated that the work is in the public domain and , therefore, it can be used freely as long as the authorship is acknowledged, provenance is mentioned and its integrity is maintained.

If you would like to know more about the terms of use for photographs and the rights governing them, please see our copyright FAQ.

COOKIE_POLICY.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
What are cookies? A cookie is a file that is downloaded to your computer when you access certain web pages. Cookies allow a website, among other things, to store and retrieve information about the browsing habits of a user or their computer and, depending on the information they contain and the way you use your computer, can be used to recognize the user.

The website http://alabern.sctestingsite.com/uses the following cookies:
COOKIE_POLICY.TABLE_ROW1_COL1: User login, authentication and language cookies
COOKIE_POLICY.TABLE_ROW1_COL3: Own management of the operation of the web portal as the user login control, cookie to accept cookies and other platform cookies for language control or the ASP.net session itself.
COOKIE_POLICY.TABLE_ROW2_COL3: This cookie generates a unique user ID and records the date, first and last time the user visited the site. It is used to count how many times a unique user visits the site.
COOKIE_POLICY.TABLE_ROW3_COL3: This cookie is used to limit the percentage of requests.
COOKIE_POLICY.TABLE_ROW3_COL4: Expires one minute after last update
COOKIE_POLICY.TABLE_ROW4_COL3: This cookie is used to distinguish between users.
COOKIE_POLICY.SECTION_3_CONTENT_HTML:
To allow, know, block or delete cookies installed on your computer you can do so by configuring the browser options installed on your computer.

You can find information on how to do this in the case you use as a browser:

ARTICLE_BLOG.FORM_NAME_MAX_LENGTH_VALIDATION: Please enter value less then 100 Char.
ARTICLE_BLOG.FORM_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH_VALIDATION: Please enter value less then 250 Char.
ARTICLE_BLOG.FORM_COMMENT_MAX_LENGTH_VALIDATION: Please enter value less then 2000 Char.

COOKIES.SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML: We use cookies to improve your browsing experience, show you personalized ads or content, and analyze our traffic. By clicking Accept all, you consent to our use of cookies.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_SECTION_1_CONTENT_HTML:
We use cookies to help you navigate efficiently and perform certain functions. You will find detailed information about each cookie under each consent category below.

We also use third-party cookies that help us analyze how you use this website, save your preferences, and deliver content and advertising that is relevant to you. These cookies are only stored in your browser with your consent.

You can choose to enable or disable some or all of these cookies, although disabling some may affect your browsing experience.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_SECTION_2_PANNEL_1_CONTENT: Necessary cookies are crucial for the basic functions of the website and the website will not function as intended without them. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_SECTION_2_PANNEL_2_CONTENT: This website may use third-party media services like YouTube or Vimeo. These services can set cookies and track your activity when you view embedded videos.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_SECTION_2_PANNEL_3_CONTENT: Les galetes analítiques s'utilitzen per comprendre com interactuen els visitants amb el lloc web. Aquestes cookies ajuden a proporcionar informació sobre mètriques com a nombre de visitants, percentatge de rebots, font de trànsit, etc.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_SECTION_2_PANNEL_4_CONTENT: Performance cookies are used to understand and analyze key website performance metrics, which helps provide a better user experience for visitors.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_SECTION_2_PANNEL_5_CONTENT: Advertising cookies are used to deliver personalized ads to visitors based on the pages they previously visited and to analyze the effectiveness of the advertising campaign.
COOKIES.COOKIE_DIALOG_NO_COOKIE: There are no cookies to display.
COOKIES.LABLE_1_DESCRIPTION: It is used to determine whether the user has accepted or rejected the cookies.
COOKIES.LABLE_2_DESCRIPTION: It is used to store language data such as name, alias, and ID.
COOKIES.LABLE_3_DESCRIPTION: It is used to store the ID of the currently active language.
COOKIES.LABLE_4_DESCRIPTION: It is used to determine whether to show the splash screen or not.
COOKIES.LABLE_5_DESCRIPTION: It is used to store the current theme of the website.
COOKIES.ANALYTICS_COOKIE_LABLE_1_DESCRIPTION: Aggregated site usage measurement. Not enabled without your permission
